test.name: Check tree attachment after extraction using record_person_matches
test.description: After a record_search result is handed over, the user asks whether the record is already attached to a tree person. Tests that record-extraction calls record_person_matches with the record persona ID and correctly reports the accepted match — informing the user the record is already linked to Patrick Flynn (LZNY-BRF) in the FamilySearch tree.

judge_context: Should call record_person_matches with id MXHY-TP4 (the record persona ID from the search result)
Should report that the record is already accepted-matched to tree person LZNY-BRF (Patrick Flynn) in the FamilySearch tree
Should still extract assertions from the record — the match check is supplemental, not a replacement for extraction
Should write assertions to research.json with record_id set to the full arkUrl and record_persona_id set to P1 for the focus person

Informant: for name/age/birthplace, use 'unknown household member' with reasoning (e.g., 'likely a parent' for a child) and proximity 'household_member'. For residence, 'census enumerator' with proximity 'witness' is acceptable. Use informant_bias_notes to explain reasoning.

Relationship assertions should be atomic — one relationship per assertion (e.g., 'child of Thomas Flynn' is one assertion). Do not compound relationship type with inference method in a single value. Relationships from the 1850 census are INDIRECT evidence (no relationship column).
